Tests the `toString` method of a `Number` object with base 36 conversion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that tests the behavior of JavaScript's `Number` object and its `toString` method. It initializes a variable `a` with the numeric value `0.1` and then converts this number to a string using base 36 representation. The `assert` function checks if the resulting string is equal to the expected value `"0.3llllllllllqsn8td1p464unmi"`. This script is likely used to verify the correctness of the `toString` method when converting numbers to a base 36 string format.
